title: stylidium willingii
intro: Stylidium willingii, also known as Willing's triggerplant, is a rare Australian native plant species belonging to the Stylidium genus. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stylidium willingii include relief from arthritis, eczema, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the roots and leaves to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
